symbols: composite head made of fire-related objects — the element of fire personified, destructive and transformative power
flames as hair — the consuming, uncontrollable nature of fire
cannons and firearms — fire harnessed as human weaponry
candles and oil lamp — fire domesticated for illumination and service
description: An allegorical composite portrait in profile view, depicting a human head and bust constructed entirely from fire-related objects. The 'hair' is a crown of blazing flames and burning twigs. The face is assembled from candles, an oil lamp forming the ear, metal flint-lock mechanisms forming the cheek and jawline. The neck and chest are composed of a cannon barrel, musket parts, and ornate metalwork with jeweled details. The entire figure glows with warm golden-orange tones against a very dark background.
